Regional Technology Summit Draws Industry Leaders

On September 12, 2023, more than two hundred executives gathered in Chicago for the annual Regional Technology Summit. The event was hosted by Meridian Systems and sponsored by Halcyon Technologies, Brightwater Capital, and the Lakeside Institute.

Maria Gonzalez, the Chief Executive Officer of Meridian Systems, opened the conference with a keynote on supply-chain automation. She was joined on stage by David Chen, the Chief Technology Officer of Halcyon Technologies, and by Senator Patricia Lowell, who spoke about regional investment.

During the morning session, Thomas Reed of Brightwater Capital announced a new fund. The fund will support startups based in Austin and Denver. Reed noted that Brightwater Capital had already backed Nimbus Solutions and Cedar Analytics during 2022.

The Lakeside Institute presented research on workforce training. Dr. Helen Park, a director at the institute, described a partnership with Riverside University that began on January 8, 2021. The program now operates from a new facility at 450 Lakeshore Avenue.

In the afternoon, a panel featured Kevin Walsh of Summit Logistics, Angela Brooks of Pioneer Industries, and Marcus Bell of Orion Group. The panel debated the future of automated warehousing. Pioneer Industries operates a distribution center at 88 Industrial Road in Cleveland.

The summit closed with remarks from Governor James Whitfield. He praised the collaboration between Meridian Systems, Halcyon Technologies, and the Lakeside Institute. Organizers confirmed that the next summit will take place on October 4, 2024.
